nativeRange.habitat: Reef-associated; adults tend to inhabit protected reef flats in lagoons and inshore reefs at shallow depths (0–4 m)

diet.notes: In the wild has been documented feeding primarily on filamentous algae and zooplankton. In aquariums has been reported to readily accept a variety of meaty and vegetable-based foods including Mysis shrimp, brine shrimp, Spirulina, frozen and freeze-dried preparations, flake, and pellet foods. Feeding multiple small portions per day is generally advised.

summary: A striking but territorial damselfish native to the Western Pacific; juveniles display vivid electric-blue stripes on black that tend to fade to uniform dark brown or black in adulthood. Generally considered suitable for beginners in semi-aggressive or predator community setups.
careNotes:
The Blue Velvet Damselfish (Neoglyphidodon oxyodon — also traded as Paraglyphidodon oxyodon, a widely-used synonym) is a hardy species generally advised for hobbyists with semi-aggressive or predator-only communities rather than peaceful reef setups. Juveniles are strikingly colored with electric-blue stripes; adults typically lose most of this coloration and have been known to become increasingly territorial and aggressive with maturity.

Water temperature: Sources varied notably. Aquarium hobby sources generally advise 22–28°C, while FishBase wild data records a preferred range of 26.5–29.3°C. The averaged target range used here (23–27°C) represents a compromise; erring toward the upper end of this range is likely closer to wild conditions.

Tank size: LiveAquaria lists 30 gallons (114 L) as a minimum while TropicalFishKeeping, AquariumDomain, and ThatPetPlace each recommend 55 gallons (208 L). The figure used here (185 L) is the average of all four; a 55-gallon (208 L) or larger tank is generally advisable, especially in community settings, to give other fish adequate space to escape territorial behavior.

Reef compatibility: Three of four sources rate this species reef-safe with respect to corals and sessile invertebrates; however, its aggressive disposition toward smaller and more peaceful fish has been noted. It is generally considered reef-safe in terms of coral/invertebrate damage, but has been known to bully and harass peaceful tankmates in reef setups.

Color phases: Juveniles are black with vivid electric-blue horizontal stripes and a pale yellow-white vertical band. Adults are typically uniform dark brown to black with minimal remaining stripe coloration.

Health: The species has been reported to be susceptible to ich, particularly under unstable water conditions. Stable parameters and consistent water quality practices are generally advised.

Lifespan: One source reports approximately 5 years; the range here (3–7 years) is a plausible estimate around that figure, as multi-source lifespan data was not available.
breedingNotes: Males have been reported to establish territories and court females with a characteristic swimming display. Spawning is oviparous; eggs are demersal and adhere to a substrate surface, with the male guarding and aerating the eggs. Captive breeding has not yet been documented in the hobby literature reviewed; this species is generally considered not bred in captivity under standard aquarium conditions.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked scientific name (Neoglyphidodon oxyodon confirmed as valid name; Paraglyphidodon oxyodon is a widely-used synonym), native range, adult size (15.0 cm), temperature (26.5–29.3°C wild preferred range), and taxonomy.

additional.name: Tropical Fish Keeping
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (6 in / 15.24 cm), temperature (72–78°F / 22.2–25.6°C), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), minimum tank size (55 gal / 208 L), lifespan (5 years), native range, breeding behavior, and temperament.

additional.name: AquariumDomain
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (6 in), temperature (72–80°F / 22.2–26.7°C), pH (8.1–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.025), minimum tank size (55 gal). Rated reef-safe 'with caution' due to aggression toward peaceful reef fish; 3 other sources rate it reef-safe outright.

additional.name: ThatPetPlace
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (6 in), temperature (75–82°F / 23.9–27.8°C), pH (8.0–8.4), salinity (1.020–1.024), minimum tank size (55 gal). Temperature range is notably higher than other hobby sources. Min salinity upper bound (1.024) slightly lower than other sources.

fish.breedingNotes: Oviparous pair spawner; eggs adhere to substrate with paternal care. Captive breeding has not been documented in the reviewed hobby literature.
